subject: DevOps
title: What Is the Software Supply Chain
description: A fast breakdown of What Is the Software Supply Chain for builders.

hook.text: In 2020, attackers slipped malware into SolarWinds' build. 18,000 customers installed poisoned updates.

buildup.text: The software supply chain spans source code, dependencies, CI/CD, and the final deployed artifact.

discovery.text: An attacker can hit any link: a rogue NPM package, a hijacked GitHub Action, or a tampered image.

twist.text: You trust your code. But do you trust every transitive dependency? Attackers exploit that blind spot.

climax.text: A 2021 US executive order mandated SBOMs for government vendors. Supply chain security is now law.

punchline.text: Your software is only as secure as its weakest link. Verify all.

quiz.question: What was notable about the SolarWinds attack?
options: Attackers compromised the build pipeline and distributed malware through legitimate updates
It was a traditional DDoS attack on servers
